heading: Transfer of excess or surplus educationally useful equipment to public schools
label: 6631 Transfer of excess or surplus educationally useful equipment to public schools
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 117b–2 of this titleSection is from the Congressional Operations Appropriations Act, 1997, which is title I of the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 1997.

children.heading: Authorization
children.label: a Authorization
children.text: The Sergeant at Arms and Doorkeeper of the Senate may directly, or through the General Services Administration, transfer title to excess or surplus educationally useful equipment to a public school. Any such transfer shall be completed at the lowest possible cost to the public school and the Senate.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: b Regulations
children.text: The Committee on Rules and Administration of the Senate shall prescribe regulations to carry out the provisions of this section.

children.heading: Deposit of receipts
children.label: c Deposit of receipts
children.text: Receipts from reimbursements for the costs of transfer of excess or surplus educationally useful equipment under this section, shall be deposited in the United States Treasury for credit to the account for the “Sergeant at Arms and Doorkeeper of the Senate” within the contingent fund of the Senate.1So in original. Comma probably should not appear.1
The term “public school” means a elementary school or secondary school, as such terms are defined in .2section 7801 of title 20So in original. Probably should be “an”.2

The term “educationally useful equipment” means computers and related peripheral tools, including printers, modems, routers, servers, computer keyboards, scanners, and other telecommunications and research equipment, that are appropriate for use in public school education.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: e Effective date
children.text: This section shall take effect beginning with fiscal year 1997 and shall be effective each fiscal year thereafter.
